cards.title: Three.js Manual
cards.why_it_matters: Canonical mental model for scenes, cameras, materials, and renderer setup in the default KMBL 3D lane.
cards.use_when: Any Three-based preview: lighting, meshes, loaders, animation loops.
cards.avoid_when: Pure 2D canvas/Pixi-first work or shader-only minimal stacks.
cards.implementation_notes: Prefer rAF loops; keep pixel ratio bounded for local preview.
cards.design_notes: Structure clarity beats shader novelty for identity sites.

cards.title: Three.js Examples
cards.why_it_matters: Shows working patterns (controls, environments, post) within the same ecosystem as the manual.
cards.use_when: Need a reference pattern close to the planned interaction (orbit controls, environments).
cards.avoid_when: Copying heavy multi-pass stacks that exceed bounded preview scope.
cards.implementation_notes: Strip to minimal subset; inline or single-script friendly shapes.
cards.design_notes: Borrow interaction feel, not asset weight.

cards.title: GSAP Documentation
cards.why_it_matters: Default motion layer paired with Three in KMBL interactive policy.
cards.use_when: UI motion, scroll-linked cues, timelines, staggers.
cards.avoid_when: Replacing all motion with WebGL when CSS/GSAP suffices.
cards.implementation_notes: CDN load + modest timelines; respect reduced-motion.
cards.design_notes: Premium feel often comes from restraint and easing, not duration alone.

cards.title: GSAP Showcase
cards.why_it_matters: Calibrates motion craft and pacing expectations for marketing-grade surfaces.
cards.use_when: Planner/generator need taste anchors for scroll/scrub narratives.
cards.avoid_when: Static editorial with no motion ambition.
cards.implementation_notes: Treat as inspiration density — ship a bounded subset.
cards.design_notes: Study sequence and hierarchy, not page length.

cards.title: WebGL Fundamentals
cards.why_it_matters: Grounds custom GL work when Three is present but shaders need clarity.
cards.use_when: Custom materials, buffer tricks, debugging GPU state alongside Three.
cards.avoid_when: Shader-first minimal lane (OGL/regl) without Three.
cards.implementation_notes: Keep shader files small; document uniforms in comments.
cards.design_notes: Visual sophistication from coherent lighting, not shader count.

cards.title: Khronos glTF Overview
cards.why_it_matters: Standard real-time asset path for Three loaders in product-style scenes.
cards.use_when: Meshes/animations delivered as glTF; PBR material expectations.
cards.avoid_when: Gaussian splat specialist lane (different asset expectations).
cards.implementation_notes: Prefer draco/compressed glTF only if decode path is documented.
cards.design_notes: Consistent materials read as higher quality than exotic geometry.

cards.title: PixiJS Guides
cards.why_it_matters: Official 2D GPU path when execution_contract escalates to Pixi.
cards.use_when: 2D-first motion, particles, canvas UI — not default 3D.
cards.avoid_when: Three-centric 3D or Gaussian splat scenes.
cards.implementation_notes: Single Application per surface; watch resolution/DPI.
cards.design_notes: Crisp 2D typography and sprite rhythm over fake 3D.

cards.title: PixiJS Examples
cards.why_it_matters: Working snippets for filters, text, and interaction within Pixi.
cards.use_when: Need a concrete pattern after choosing Pixi escalation.
cards.avoid_when: Default Three+GSAP lane suffices.
cards.implementation_notes: Keep filter stack shallow for preview FPS.
cards.design_notes: Motion reads cleaner with fewer simultaneous effects.

cards.title: OGL (minimal WebGL)
cards.why_it_matters: Shader-first minimal abstraction when Three is intentionally avoided.
cards.use_when: Small custom renderer, tight GL control, art experiments.
cards.avoid_when: Stock marketing components — use default Three+GSAP.
cards.implementation_notes: Pair with small glsl/vert/frag files; few draw calls.
cards.design_notes: Minimal renderers shine with strong art direction.

cards.title: TWGL.js
cards.why_it_matters: Thin helpers for raw WebGL when regl/OGL choices are too opinionated.
cards.use_when: Explicit GL state, tutorials aligned to raw WebGL.
cards.avoid_when: Could ship faster with Three default lane.
cards.implementation_notes: Keep attribute/uniform layout documented.
cards.design_notes: Shader readability is part of UX for technical brands.

cards.title: regl functional WebGL
cards.why_it_matters: Alternative minimal GL style; useful when functional draw DSL fits.
cards.use_when: Pass-heavy GL with clear draw command structure.
cards.avoid_when: Need scene-graph ergonomics — prefer Three.
cards.implementation_notes: Cap draw passes for preview; avoid huge textures.
cards.design_notes: Consistency in passes beats one-off shader tricks.

cards.title: WebGPU Fundamentals
cards.why_it_matters: WGSL/WebGPU literacy when experience_mode or libraries include wgsl.
cards.use_when: Heavy GPU ambition, compute-ish effects, Three WebGPU path.
cards.avoid_when: Flat static sites or WebGL-sufficient effects.
cards.implementation_notes: Branch on navigator.gpu; ship honest fallback UI.
cards.design_notes: Advanced GPU should still feel intentional, not experimental noise.

cards.title: MDN WebGPU API
cards.why_it_matters: Browser capability and API surface reference for guarded WebGPU use.
cards.use_when: Checking feature availability and core API naming.
cards.avoid_when: Teaching art direction — use design references instead.
cards.implementation_notes: Combine with fundamentals for working samples.
cards.design_notes: Fallback messaging is a design choice — write it clearly.

cards.title: gaussian-splats-3d (Three-compatible)
cards.why_it_matters: KMBL preferred splat viewer token — Three-friendly, preview-oriented.
cards.use_when: Photoreal captured scenes, scanned spaces, splat storytelling — with gaussian_splat_v1 escalation.
cards.avoid_when: Generic hero sites or mesh-solvable 3D.
cards.implementation_notes: Expect .splat/.ply assets under component/assets; lazy-load; UI fallback if missing.
cards.design_notes: Splat novelty must serve narrative — anchor with typography and pacing.

cards.title: Awwwards
cards.why_it_matters: Benchmark for premium web craft, pacing, and visual hierarchy.
cards.use_when: Planner/generator calibrating high-end marketing or portfolio tone.
cards.avoid_when: Need implementation API docs — use official library refs.
cards.implementation_notes: Never scrape; cite URLs only as taste anchors.
cards.design_notes: Study composition, scroll rhythm, and restraint — not feature parity.

cards.title: Codrops
cards.why_it_matters: Editorial motion and interaction ideas grounded in web tech.
cards.use_when: Looking for scroll, hover, and transition craft patterns.
cards.avoid_when: Copying full demos verbatim — adapt to bounded preview.
cards.implementation_notes: Port ideas to GSAP/CSS/Three minimally.
cards.design_notes: Clarity beats spectacle for identity-critical pages.

cards.title: siteInspire
cards.why_it_matters: Curated layouts and brand-forward composition references.
cards.use_when: Shaping grid, typography, and section flow for premium feel.
cards.avoid_when: Deep WebGL engineering questions.
cards.implementation_notes: Use as composition brief signals, not asset sources.
cards.design_notes: Whitespace and type scale carry luxury as much as motion.

cards.title: Hover States
cards.why_it_matters: Micro-interaction literacy for polished UI affordances.
cards.use_when: Buttons, cursors, cards, and small motion details.
cards.avoid_when: Immersive 3D world-building — switch to Three/splat refs.
cards.implementation_notes: Keep micro-motion under ~300ms for preview snappiness.
cards.design_notes: Feedback should reinforce hierarchy, not decorate randomly.

cards.title: Readymag: sites we like
cards.why_it_matters: Editorial and visual-storytelling references adjacent to interactive publishing.
cards.use_when: Long-form identity storytelling with strong art direction.
cards.avoid_when: Need shader/API specifics.
cards.implementation_notes: Borrow narrative pacing, not proprietary tool features.
cards.design_notes: Rhythm between text and visual beats defines premium editorial.

cards.title: Linear — craft articles
cards.why_it_matters: Reference for crisp product UI, motion discipline, and polish bar.
cards.use_when: B2B/SaaS identity with high perceived quality.
cards.avoid_when: Playful illustrative brands needing organic chaos.
cards.implementation_notes: Emulate clarity and timing, not proprietary components.
cards.design_notes: Precision typography + subtle motion reads as premium.
